summary: Implemented the Stage 7F cleanup-plan dry-run CLI, exposing non-destructive cleanup planning through JSON and human-readable output while preserving containment and active-run guards.

tests_run.command: uv run --no-sync python -B -m pytest tests/test_worktree_cleanup.py -q -p no:cacheprovider

tests_run.summary: 292 passed plus Ruff, format, mypy, CLI smoke checks, file justification, public hygiene, planning integrity, skill inventory, source inventory, protected locks, and uv lock --check.

cleanup-plan CLI exposes non-destructive.evidence: codex-supervisor cleanup-plan accepts --workspace-root, repeated --candidate, repeated --active-worker-run-id, --reason, and --json, then returns the CleanupPlan dataclass as structured JSON.

The CLI delegates containment and runtim.evidence: The CLI handler calls plan_cleanup_targets and catches WorktreeArtifactError, returning exit code 1; tests verify invalid outside candidates fail and valid candidate directories remain on disk.

Human-readable output distinguishes sele.evidence: Human output prints selected and skipped sections with structured operation labels, and focused tests assert it does not include Remove-Item or rm shell commands.

Focused tests cover JSON output, active-.evidence: tests/test_worktree_cleanup.py covers JSON output, active worker-run skipping, invalid candidate failure, human output, and post-plan directory existence.

risks: cleanup-plan is still dry-run only; no live deletion or git worktree prune/remove behavior exists.
The command requires caller-supplied candidates and active worker-run IDs; automatic discovery from planning SQLite can be added later.
A future live deletion command should require an explicit approval/review gate and consume only selected CleanupPlan entries.
follow_up_tasks: Move to Stage 8 review and verification loop tasks, or add a later integration slice that feeds active worker-run IDs into cleanup-plan from planning SQLite.
Keep any live cleanup implementation separate from dry-run planning and guarded by validate_cleanup_target.
handoff_notes: Stage 7F adds the cleanup-plan dry-run CLI. Stage 7 now has path layout, launch preparation, orchestration changed-path gates, worktree state snapshots, cleanup planning, and a non-destructive cleanup command surface.
